search_queries_used: site:entnet.org tonsillectomy children tonsillar hypertrophy sleep-disordered breathing comorbidities
candidate_sources_rejected: tonsil size treated as sleep apnoea or surgical indication
paediatric evidence applied automatically to adults
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Tonsil appearance alone does not establish diagnosis, severity, or treatment need.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Children aged 1 to 18 with clinician-assessed tonsillar findings; adult tonsil assessment requires separate evidence.
setting_applicability: Primary, urgent, audiology, respiratory, sleep, or ENT assessment as qualified by the reviewed source and workflow purpose.
UAE_applicability: International evidence requires UAE ENT, audiology, sleep-service, emergency, prescribing, referral, and local pathway adaptation.
recency_verification: Exact official sections and current source pages were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The cited official source remains current on its issuing organisation website at the review date.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact paediatric section supports documenting caregiver-reported sleep symptoms, functional or behavioural impact, relevant negatives, ENT examination and comorbidity context without diagnosing tonsillar hypertrophy or recommending surgery.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting sleep symptoms and selected comorbidity context in children without diagnosing obstructive sleep apnoea or ordering polysomnography.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact recurrent-infection section supports clinician-entered review documentation without applying a tonsillectomy threshold.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting recurrent clinically significant throat infections, episode-associated fever, cervical nodes, exudate or testing, functional impact, and modifying factors without applying an automatic surgery threshold.

unresolved_source_gaps: Adult population, grading method, asymmetry, acute infection, airway status, diagnosis, surgery, testing, and follow-up interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
